Patch Set 7: Patch Set 6 was rebased

Patch Set 7:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/23291/

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

This needs to be broken up into multiple patches:

1)  A patch to eliminate the dlopen of a hard-coded library 
version.  The benefit here is that this can easily be backported to 2.4 and 2.5 if we so choose (LLNL will certainly want to apply this patch to its 2.4 branch).

2)  A patch to deal with the dsl_sync_task_do_nowait() change.  This patch should involve an autoconf test to determine which style of the function to employ.  Do not gratuitously break compatibility!

3)  An lbuild patch.  When and where you decided to update the master branch to a different version of zfs is independant of the previous two fixes, and should be handled separately.

Patch Set 7:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 7: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/23291/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

This needs to be broken up into multiple patches:

1) A patch to eliminate the dlopen of a hard-coded library version. The benefit here is that this can easily be backported to 2.4 and 2.5 if we so choose (LLNL will certainly want to apply this patch to its 2.4 branch).

2) A patch to deal with the dsl_sync_task_do_nowait() change. This patch should involve an autoconf test to determine which style of the function to employ. Do not gratuitously break compatibility!

3) An lbuild patch. When and where you decided to update the master branch to a different version of zfs is independant of the previous two fixes, and should be handled separately.

Patch Set 7:

Christopher:

1) I'm not sure how to accomplish that.  Currently zfs doesn't install a non-versioned .so, (eg libzfs.so) and my understanding is that if we don't use dlopen, then mount.lustre will have a shared library dependency on libzfs and rpm will create a zfs dependency for the standard lustre package (which I understand would be a pretty big blocker).

2) I added this to be expedient to get a build done to run tests on.  Alex has actually done a "real" patch for this: http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/10064 cf LU-4944

3) What is in lbuild (prior to this patch) can/will be updated to 0.6.3 since it's a pointer to a branch in the local git repo, that is currently just sitting at 0.6.2.  When 0.6.3 comes out, that branch pointer can just be moved so there would be no need to make an lbuild change to update to new zfs/spl release (for good or ill).

Patch Set 7:

Lets take the discussion about the dlopen issue back to LU-4606.  It sounds like this will require some back-and forth.

Patch Set 11: Verified-1

libtool is linking the system libzfs.so (which is from zfs-0.6.2 and is libzfs.so.1) so the zfs interace for mkfs and mount is trying to load the wrong version of libzfs.so and it is failing to create zfs filesystems.

Patch Set 11:

Do we need to uninstall the system {spl,zfs}-devel libraries so that they are not being linked incorrectly?

Patch Set 11:

There's some weirdism in the build system that I'm having trouble reproducing locally.  Given the arguments we're passing it should correctly link to libzfs.so.2, BUT the linker on the build system is being passed the following:
"libtool: link: ccache gcc -shared  .libs/mount_osd_zfs_la-mount_utils_zfs.o   -Wl,-rpath -Wl,//lib64 -Wl,-rpath -Wl,//lib64 //lib64/libzfs.so -L//lib64 -L/var/lib/jenkins/workspace/lustre-reviews/arch/x86_64/build_type/server/distro/el6/ib_stack/inkernel/BUILD/usr/lib64 -L/var/lib/jenkins/workspace/lustre-reviews/arch/x86_64/build_type/server/distro/el6/ib_stack/inkernel/BUILD/usr/lib -L/var/lib/jenkins/workspace/lustre-reviews/arch/x86_64/build_type/server/distro/el6/ib_stack/inkernel/BUILD/lib64 -L/var/lib/jenkins/workspace/lustre-reviews/arch/x86_64/build_type/server/distro/el6/ib_stack/inkernel/BUILD/lib -lkeyutils  -pthread   -pthread -Wl,-soname -Wl,mount_osd_zfs.so -o .libs/mount_osd_zfs.so"  Notice the //lib64/libzfs.so

I haven't been able to figure out where those linker options are coming from, maybe ccache?

Patch Set 12:

I was also having problems with the build of ZFS on my local test system reporting an empty path:

 libtool: link: gcc -g -Wall -fPIC -D_GNU_SOURCE -g -O2 -Werror -o req_layout req-layout.o  -L/usr/src/lustre-head/lnet/utils -lkeyutils
 /bin/grep: //lib64/libefi.la: No such file or directory
 /bin/sed: can't read //lib64/libefi.la: No such file or directory
 libtool: link: `//lib64/libefi.la' is not a valid libtool archive
 make[4]: *** [mount_osd_zfs.la] Error 1


I suspect the root of the problem was the introduction of libtool into the build system in http://review.whamcloud.com/10193 but unfortunately, it doesn't seem that this patch fixed the problem for me.

Patch Set 12: Verified-1

same issue as before, trying to reproduce locally via lbuild

parents.subject: Revert "LU-4398 mdt: acquire an open lock for write or execute"

commit.subject: LU-5203 zfs: Update spl/zfs version to 0.6.3
commit.message:
LU-5203 zfs: Update spl/zfs version to 0.6.3

Update SPL and ZFS version to official 0.6.3.
This also fixes the "search path" for libzfs.so.

Test-Parameters: mdsfilesystemtype=zfs  mdtfilesystemtype=zfs ostfilesystemtype=zfs testgroup=review-zfs
